parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-184 (RP05 Matter Core, Risk C, 150 units, RP05.P04.M06.S16–RP05.P05.M05.S13). The pack-level production_ready_candidate claim is supported and correctly gated: implementation is descriptor-only with no-write/no-real-data boundaries embedded at code level (freeze helpers in service.js) and enforced by validator assertions (validators.js createMatterCoreCp184* family) and 4 CP184 tests; the local validator (scripts/validate-rp05-matter-core-contract.mjs) and all 7 command-evidence commands exit 0 (node --check x5, node --test, npm run rp05:matter-core:validate), asserting the fixture-evidence terminal descriptor (132 P05 entry cases across 6 surfaces x 22 case types, 12 golden, 24 failure, 30 fixtures) with all six no-leak guards true and handoff to CP00-185/RP05.P05.M05.S14. Crucially, production_ready is false everywhere (manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, adjudication) with only production_ready_candidate true — the pack does NOT prematurely claim production readiness or enterprise trust. CP178+ review hardening preconditions hold: readiness auth_preflight passed (stdout 1457 bytes, valid JSON result, not 0-byte/auth-failure/malformed), validation-report shows prompt within size limit, no forbidden tools, no omitted active untracked implementation files, and review schema present; this review used only Read/Grep/Glob and emits schema-conformant JSON. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claude_not_final_approver=true, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic control-plane evidence/packet/receipt handling (hermes_ui_evidence descriptors, hermes_gate H05, createMatterCore*HermesEvidencePacket) and never for product-runtime execution (no_loop_engine, no_citation_ledger_runtime, no_graph_provider_runtime all true). No P0 or P1 issues found. One P3 cosmetic observation: case_type 'failure_test' is grouped into the failure-case set while its per-case fixture outcome derives to 'passed'. AUTHORITY BOUNDARY: this PASS is an independent review signal only; per the pack authority boundary (claude_is_final_approver=false, human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim=true), final closeout still requires the deterministic Hermes H05 gate and human final approval — those gates remain open and are out of scope for this read-only review.

findings.title: case_type 'failure_test' grouped in failure-case set but its per-case fixture outcome derives to 'passed'
findings.details: In the P05 entry fixture-case builder (p05EntryFixtureCase, ~line 724), the per-case outcome is computed as denied?'blocked':reviewRequired?'review_required':'passed'. The case_type 'failure_test' is neither a denied nor review-required type, so its individual outcome resolves to 'passed', yet 'failure_test' is included in the failureCases grouping (filter over ['denied_case','cross_tenant_case','missing_context_case','failure_test']) used to derive the failure_count of 24 in createMatterCoreFixtureEvidenceTerminalDescriptor. This is an internal naming/grouping nuance only: the aggregate invariants the validator and tests assert (132 total P05 cases, 12 golden, 24 failure, 30 fixtures, all six no-leak guards true) are all consistent and pass, and no leak-safety, no-write, or authority-boundary property is affected.
findings.recommendation: Non-blocking. Optionally either (a) rename the 'failure_test' case_type to clarify it is a fixture/test-harness case rather than an outcome-failure case, or (b) add a one-line comment at the outcome derivation noting that grouping membership (failure_case_set) is intentionally independent of the derived per-case outcome. No change is required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as cosmetic and non-blocking. Does not affect finding_counts gating, does not block pack or goal closeout, and leaves no unresolved P0/P1. May be deferred to a future RP05.P05 entry pack or left as-is.
